mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances despite higher rates.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where payroll growth exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer demand strength, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and GDPC1 yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and reduced corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicates a policy lag or economic deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases MoM while FEDFUNDS does not decrease
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.6 million units annually signal strong construction sector demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 1.6M are followed by negative 60-day returns in > 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings above 200 on the CPI index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 200.0 is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate reflects improving labor market tightness and rising wage pressures that boost consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Falling UNRATE values are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of monthly observations.
